commands.review_summary:
Adds purpose-specific memory role slots, per-agent memory slot overrides, legacy memory-slot migration, and role-aware memory/plugin/runtime/docs/tests support.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/config architecture PR, not a report of broken current-main behavior.
commands.review_followup: Manual maintainer review is needed for the public memory role taxonomy, migration contract, and final proof-head note; there is no narrow automated repair candidate.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Refreshed final end-to-end proof for head `7c0b78f1b36890b6348242324f71577d2291f8a0`, including the post-proof legacy `registerMemoryPromptSection` fallback repair, granular memory role slots, legacy `plugins.slots.memory` upgrade handling, per-agent memory-only overrides, LosslessClaw as global `contextEngine`, Honcho/QMD-backed recall paths, dreaming/compaction/userModel/capture role activation, wiki/public artifact handling, disabled recall, and loader/cache-sensitive per-agent slot behavior.
- **Real environment tested:** Dev-01 source checkout at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/workspace/repos/openclaw`; Orion/Nano-01 proof checkout at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210`; Node `v24.15.0`; `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`; self-hosted Honcho at `127.0.0.1:8000`; LosslessClaw configured as `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; redacted artifacts under `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210/.artifacts/proof-refresh-7c0b78f1-20260525T221705Z`.
- **Exact steps or command run after fix:** Rolled Orion back to old base `0f35ec29d34989a8e957cbc59607b261cf5d6695`, before this PR's slot schema/code; stripped granular `memory.*` slot keys so old OpenClaw saw legacy `plugins.slots.memory = "memory-core"`; ran old `config validate` and `doctor`; upgraded the same config home to PR head; ran `doctor --fix` and `config validate`; ran focused memory/plugin/config/doctor/runtime/CLI prompt Vitest suites; ran the agent shard; ran lint, type, diff, direct model, live model, live gateway prompt, Honcho status/ask, deterministic five-plugin role routing, wiki artifact, legacy prompt fallback, and five live agent prompt proofs.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted runtime logs and JSON artifacts are indexed at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210/.artifacts/proof-refresh-7c0b78f1-20260525T221705Z/redacted/99-final-refresh-summary.json`; copied live output markers include `OPENCLAW_PR86210_7C0B78F1_OK`, `LOCAL_HONCHO_7C0B78F1_OK`, `LEGACY_PROMPT_FALLBACK_OK`, and `AGENT_*_7C0B78F1_OK` for all five proof agents. Key proof files include `redacted/13-upgraded-config-summary.json`, `redacted/40-memory-slot-proof.json`, `redacted/41-wiki-artifact-proof.json`, `redacted/42-legacy-prompt-fallback-proof.json`, and `redacted/50-agent-*.json`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Legacy config upgraded cleanly, canonical `memory.recall` was added while legacy `memory` remained compatible, role-slot routing selected the expected plugins per agent, disabled recall suppressed recall artifacts/prompt fallback, wiki artifacts followed active recall, Honcho/QMD and LosslessClaw paths initialized, and live direct/gateway model plus five configured agent prompts returned the expected markers.
- **Upgrade evidence:** Old stripped config validated on pre-PR code, then PR `doctor --fix` copied legacy `plugins.slots.memory` to canonical `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` while retaining the legacy key for compatibility. Artifacts: `redacted/04-old-stripped-config-summary.json`, `redacted/05-old-config-validate-rerun.log`, `redacted/06-old-doctor-preview-rerun.log`, `redacted/11-pr-doctor-fix.log`, `redacted/12-pr-config-validate-after.log`, `redacted/13-upgraded-config-summary.json`.
- **Deterministic evidence:** Focused PR suite passed, including `memory-state`, `memory-runtime`, `loader`, `memory-wiki`, hooks/skills, effective plugin IDs, uninstall/update/channel planning, doctor legacy migration/stale config, slots, and `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` for legacy prompt compatibility. Sequential lint shards passed after Orion killed the parallel `pnpm lint` process under memory pressure: `22a-lint-scripts`, `22b-lint-core`, `22c-lint-extensions`; `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json`, `tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`, and `git diff --check` passed.
- **Role-slot evidence:** Five proof memory plugins loaded: `proof-core`, `proof-honcho`, `proof-capture`, `proof-dream`, and `proof-compact`. Routing result: `main -> proof-core`, `honcho-agent -> proof-honcho`, `dreamer-agent -> proof-core`, `disabled-recall-agent -> <none>`, and `split-agent -> proof-core`; compaction routed through `proof-compact-provider`; capture, dreaming, userModel, recall, and compaction activation metadata were recorded. Artifacts: `redacted/40-memory-slot-proof.json`, `redacted/41-wiki-artifact-proof.json`.
- **Legacy prompt fallback evidence:** New proof `redacted/42-legacy-prompt-fallback-proof.json` shows config-aware prompt building falls back to the legacy `legacy-memory-v1` registration when `memory.recall` selects a plugin without a capability, and still returns no prompt/capability when `memory.recall = "none"`.
- **Live evidence:** Direct model proof returned `OPENCLAW_PR86210_7C0B78F1_OK`; broad live direct model suite passed against stripped model config; live gateway prompt/tool/image path completed for `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`; Honcho status/ask passed and returned `LOCAL_HONCHO_7C0B78F1_OK`; five configured live agents returned exact markers: `AGENT_main_7C0B78F1_OK`, `AGENT_honcho-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`, `AGENT_dreamer-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`, `AGENT_disabled-recall-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`, and `AGENT_split-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`. Artifacts: `redacted/30-live-config-summary.json`, `redacted/30b-broad-model-config-summary.json`, `redacted/32-models-status.json`, `redacted/33-model-run.json`, `redacted/34c-live-models-broad-model-home.log`, `redacted/35c-live-gateway-models-broad-model-home-timeout300.log`, `redacted/36-honcho-status.log`, `redacted/37-honcho-ask.log`, `redacted/50-agent-*.json`.
- **Known unrelated Orion baselines:** `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` fails on both PR head and upstream-main old base in the Orion checkout; the gateway live suite's static auth-sanitizer assertion also fails on upstream-main. Both comparisons are captured as `redacted/21b-code-mode-rerun.log`, `redacted/21c-code-mode-upstream-main.log`, and `redacted/35d-gateway-auth-sanitizer-upstream-main.log`. The PR-relevant CLI prompt, memory routing, direct model, live gateway prompt/tool/image, Honcho, wiki, and five-agent proofs passed.
- **Final artifact index:** `redacted/99-final-refresh-summary.json`.
- **What was not tested:** Hosted `api.honcho.dev` was not used; Honcho proof is self-hosted/local on Orion. The future owner-provenance object shape from #70823 remains out of scope.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current pushed head:

- `788fef03e308b954c9322d5b5d8f9f35024493a5` (`fix(config): hide internal config rollback symbol`)

Local/Orion gates from the refreshed head:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` on old base and PR head: passed.
- `corepack pnpm build:strict-smoke` on old base and PR head: passed.
- Old-base stripped config `config validate` and `doctor`: passed.
- PR-head upgraded config `doctor`, `doctor --fix`, and `config validate`: passed.
- Focused memory/plugin/config/doctor/runtime Vitest suite: passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json`: passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- Five-agent memory role live prompt proof: passed.
- Direct live model proof: passed.
- Live model suite: 17 tests passed.
- Live gateway model suite: 60 tests passed.
- Self-hosted Honcho status/ask proof: passed.
- LosslessClaw context-engine startup/migration proof: captured in redacted live logs.

GitHub Actions for pushed head `788fef03e308b954c9322d5b5d8f9f35024493a5` are expected to run after this body refresh.
target.author: kklouzal
